text: I have invented this loop passer to perform various assisted two-port appendectomy. You see there is a groove at one end to accommodate the thread loop. I have used this hand quilting needle that is having an eye at one end of the length of six point five centimeter and shaved off the half length of this eye to make a groove and then joined these two needles with each other to make this full length low passer of thirteen centimeter that will be accommodated in twelve centimeter various needle

The surgeon removes the inner stylet of the varice needle, leaving only the outer needle. A full-length thread is looped at the center and fed into the groove of the loop passer. The surgeon holds both ends of the thread with the right hand and inserts the loop passer into the upper end of the needle, allowing it to emerge from the lower sharp end. Using a grasper, the loop is grasped intraperitoneally, and the loop passer is removed.

and then you can feed the tip of the appendix through this loop and perform your maneuvers now i will demonstrate all these steps on a dummy to make your concepts clear but for live demonstration of this procedure there is a video link in the description kindly visit that link

The surgeon inserts a 10 mm optical trocar in the inferior umbilical crease and a 5 mm trocar in the suprapubic region. The varus needle is inserted at a point in the right iliac fossa, allowing for optimal handling of the appendix.

The surgeon removes the inner stylet from the varus needle and feeds a loop into the groove of the loop passer. Using artery forceps, the loop is inserted into the varus needle until it extends into the peritoneal cavity, after which the loop passer is withdrawn.

The surgeon uses a grasper to pull extra length of the loop inside the peritoneal cavity. The tip of the appendix is held with the grasper and the loop is slid over it using the varus needle and grasper. The loop is tightened by pulling it outside, allowing manipulation of the appendix tip in various directions.

The surgeon works on the meso-appendix, using either a ligature or an extracorporeal knot for dissection. If the appendix is long, the loop around it is loosened to hold the appendix further proximally for easier handling and manipulation. Once the dissection is complete, the loop is removed and extracted from the varus needle.
